chapter_title.ar: بَابُ حَجِّ الْمُجَاوِرِينَ وَ قُطَّانِ مَكَّةَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 57 – The Hajj of the ones in the vicinity and residents of Makkah

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/206)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/206)
text: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ الْمُجَاوِرُ بِمَكَّةَ سَنَةً يَعْمَلُ عَمَلَ أَهْلِ مَكَّةَ يَعْنِي يُفْرِدُ الْحَجَّ مَعَ أَهْلِ مَكَّةَ وَ مَا كَانَ دُونَ السَّنَةِ فَلَهُ أَنْ يَتَمَتَّعَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Biin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ismail Bin Marrar, from Yunus, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I heard himasws saying: ‘The one in the vicinity of Makkah for a year should be doing the deed of the people of Makkah, meaning he would be performing the exclusive Hajj along with the people of Makkah, and whatever was besides the year, so for him is that he should not be performing Tamatto’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Isma‘il ibn Marrar from Yunus from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘One who resides in Makkah for one year is like the people of Makkah, thus, he must perform al-Hajj in Ifrad manner (al-Hajj without al-‘Umrah) along with the people of Makkah. However, if one resides in Makkah for less than one year, he is required to perform al-Hajj al-Tamattu‘ (advantageous).’”
